plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% for better single-name diversification.
Buy XLV at 8% to add a defensive sector to the portfolio.
Increase cash to 17% for a substantial safety buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces overall risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and increasing cash, but potential upside is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income and broad exposure.
Add to CRWD, bringing it to 15% to lean into quality tech.
Buy RTX at 10% as a strong new diversified setup.
Hold 10% cash to maintain a prudent buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month by focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration, blending broad market exposure with quality tech, while maintaining a moderate cash buffer.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit losing leveraged positions.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a winning AI/Software name.
Buy AMAT at 15% as a high-scoring semiconductor setup.
Buy RTX at 15% as another high-scoring diversified setup.
Maintain a small 5% cash buffer to maximize market exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by stretching growth and leaning into high-scoring setups. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk, especially given the rapid decay and rebalancing of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the amplified risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income via covered calls.
Sell CRWD and SMH to exit high-beta single names and sectors.
Maintain a large 15% cash buffer for stability and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month by prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It rotates heavily into income-focused ETFs and broad index, significantly reducing leverage and high-beta exposure. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
